R-SLA-01.name: P0 Critical Value SLA
R-SLA-01.description: P0_critical cases must be routed within 15 minutes

R-SLA-02.name: P1 Urgent SLA
R-SLA-02.description: P1_urgent cases must be routed within 60 minutes

R-SLA-03.name: Result TAT Window
R-SLA-03.description: Guardant360 standard TAT = 7 business days; SHIELD = 10 days

R-ELG-01.name: Re-draw Eligibility
R-ELG-01.description: Re-draw eligible if: < 30 days since original draw AND patient has valid active insurance

R-ELG-02.name: PA Required Check
R-ELG-02.description: PA required for all commercial payer orders > $3,000 list price

R-ELG-03.name: Critical Value — SHIELD
R-ELG-03.description: SHIELD positive signal qualifies as P0_critical requiring physician acknowledgment within 24 hours

R-ELG-04.name: Critical Value — G360 Actionable Mutation
R-ELG-04.description: G360 with actionable mutation (EGFR, ALK, ROS1, BRAF, KRAS G12C) qualifies as P1_urgent minimum

R-ORD-01.name: Required Order Fields
R-ORD-01.description: Required: patient_name, DOB, ordering_physician_NPI, ICD-10_code, specimen_type, insurance_id

R-ORD-02.name: Physician NPI Validation
R-ORD-02.description: Ordering physician NPI must be 10 digits

R-AUT-01.name: Auto-Resolution Gate
R-AUT-01.description: auto_resolvable = true ONLY IF: all confidences >= 0.80 AND case_type != P0_critical AND no critical rules failed

R-AUT-02.name: P0 Hard Stop
R-AUT-02.description: P0_critical cases NEVER auto-resolve regardless of any other factor

R-CNF-01.name: Low Confidence Escalation
R-CNF-01.description: If any single step confidence < 0.75, case requires human review

R-PA-01.name: PA Stale Status
R-PA-01.description: PA submitted > 10 business days ago with no status update is considered stale

R-PA-02.name: PA Denial Appeal Window
R-PA-02.description: PA denial must be appealed within 60 days of denial date

R-BST-01.name: Batch Order Size Cap
R-BST-01.description: Batch uploads > 50 orders routed to order_management regardless of content
